title: The Lord Seated upon the Colt
subtitle: Christ, the true Lamb, sets out for Jerusalem upon a borrowed beast

stillness: Be still, and let the road to Jerusalem open before you.
The time draws near when He wills to give the world its remedy by the pouring out of His own blood.
Wait here, quietly, before He sets out.
gospel: The Lord came to Bethphage, at the Mount of Olives.
And when they drew near to Jerusalem,
then Jesus sent two of His disciples,
and said to them: Go into the village over against you;
and immediately you shall find an ass tied, and a colt with her,
on whom no man yet has sat.
Loose them, and bring them to me.
And if any man shall say anything to you,
say that the Lord has need of them;
and forthwith he will let them go.
behold: The mysteries were thronging, the Scriptures were being fulfilled through the Lord Jesus.
And so, on the morrow, on the Lord's day, at the height of the morning,
He made Himself ready to go up into Jerusalem, in a new and unwonted manner, yet as it had been foretold.
The paschal time pressing on, when the lamb was slain,
He, as the true Lamb, drew near to the place of His Passion, to be offered willingly for the salvation of the world.
By the deed He shows that He was most ready to humble Himself, and to obey even unto the most contemptible and most bitter death, for all.
And His Mother, as is believed, out of love held Him back;
and to the disciples, and to others, it seemed unbearable that He should go thither, and as they could, they drew Him back.
O God! how they loved Him; and how bitter to them was whatever might wound Him!
But He had ordained otherwise, who thirsted for the salvation of all.
He began therefore to go, and that little but faithful company of disciples to walk on after Him.
Behold then how lowly and contemptible a beast Christ, the King of kings, chose to sit upon, when He would enter Jerusalem.
consider: It was the precept of the Law that the lamb be shut up in the house from the tenth day until it was slain at evening.

So the true Lamb, chosen out of the whole flock without stain, to be slain for the salvation and sanctifying of the people, went up to Jerusalem five days before, that the truth might answer to the figure.
In that the Lord came to the Mount of Olives, it is signified that He came to the Passion by love alone, and not by necessity.
This mount is called the mount of oil, of chrism, and of light.
In the oil, the pardon of forgiveness; in the chrism, the anointing of grace; in the light, the splendour of glory.
An ass, and a colt with her, on whom no man yet has sat, bound in a public place, and set apart for the service of the poor.
See then how lowly and contemptible a beast the King of kings chose to sit upon.
The virtue of Christ moved and made ready the heart of their keeper, that at once, at the word of the disciples, he gave the beasts to them, though they were strangers.
Mystically, the ass is the Jewish people for their folly; the colt the people of the Gentiles for their wantonness and their custom of sinning.
And He, who thirsted for the salvation of all, would mount them both.
prayer: Lord Jesus Christ, who came of Your own will to the Passion,
come also to me, a wretched one, by grace into my heart;
and who then restored me by the cross, restore me, fallen again, by that same blessed Passion.
Grant me, Lord, that in all things I may keep humility and meekness,
and may subject to You wholly my flesh and my spirit,
and that I may become as a beast of burden before You;
that, seated in me, and ruling me as Your beast,
You may lead me now to the taste of inward peace, and the desire of the peace above,
and at last bring me into the vision of that same peace. Amen.

latinJewel.latin: Ipse vero aliter disposuerat, qui universorum salutem sitiebat.
latinJewel.english: But He had ordained otherwise, He who thirsted for the salvation of all.
